title: Anger Episode Log
description: Track your anger episodes to identify patterns in triggers, warning signs, thoughts, and behaviors. Over time, this log will reveal what sets you off most, when you're most vulnerable, and where in the escalation chain you have the most room to intervene.
instructions: Each time you experience noticeable anger (roughly a 4+ on a 0-10 scale), log it here as soon as you can afterward. The more honestly you record, the more useful your patterns will become.

fields.label: What happened? (Who, what, where)
fields.placeholder: e.g., My partner criticized how I loaded the dishwasher in front of their parents

options.label: Last straw — many small things building up

fields.label: Body warning signs you noticed
fields.placeholder: e.g., Clenched jaw, heat in face, tight fists, racing heart

fields.label: The main thought fuelling your anger
fields.placeholder: e.g., 'They're doing this on purpose to humiliate me', 'They should know better', 'I can't stand this'

fields.label: Peak anger intensity (0 = calm, 10 = most angry ever)
What did you do? (Your actual response)
fields.placeholder: e.g., Raised my voice, walked away, used breathing, said something hurtful, took a time-out

fields.label: What happened afterward? How did you feel later?
fields.placeholder: e.g., We didn't speak for the rest of the evening. I felt guilty and frustrated with myself. We talked the next morning.
